mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in retail sales (RSAFS) indicates robust consumer demand and confidence, which directly translates into higher corporate revenues and earnings, thereby driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with RSAFS month-over-month growth over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant decline in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) below a key psychological threshold signals a tightening labor market, which boosts consumer spending power and confidence, leading to improved corporate profits and positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE drops below 9.5% and stays below for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are negative or flat in more than 60% of instances where UNRATE falls below 9.5% and remains there.

mechanism_narrative: When both the Federal Funds Rate (FEDFUNDS) is kept low and inflation (CPIAUCSL) remains contained, it creates an accommodative monetary environment. This reduces the cost of capital for businesses and lowers the discount rate for future earnings, supporting higher equity valuations and positive S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are negative or flat in more than 50% of periods where both FEDFUNDS is low and CPIAUCSL growth is contained.

mechanism_narrative: Robust and accelerating industrial production (INDPRO) signals strong manufacturing and supply-side activity, indicating healthy corporate order books and capacity utilization. This leads to improved earnings prospects for companies, driving positive S&P 500 returns, particularly during economic expansions.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are negative despite INDPRO 3-month average growth exceeding 0.5% during confirmed economic expansion periods.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in housing starts (HOUST) above a critical level indicates a strengthening housing market and broader economic confidence. This boosts demand for construction-related goods and services, contributing to overall economic growth and positive S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: HOUST > 600 (thousands of units) for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are negative or flat in more than 55% of periods where HOUST exceeds 600 for two consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in non-farm payrolls (PAYEMS) signifies a healthy and expanding labor market. This leads to increased aggregate income and consumer spending, directly supporting corporate revenues and driving positive S&P 500 returns over a very short horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with PAYEMS month-over-month growth over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of monetary tightening, a significant increase in the Federal Funds Rate (FEDFUNDS) raises borrowing costs for businesses and consumers, dampening economic activity and corporate profitability. This leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises by > 0.25% over a 3-month period
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive or flat in more than 60% of instances where FEDFUNDS rises by > 0.25% over 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP (GDPC1) growth is strong and the unemployment rate (UNRATE) is falling, it indicates a robust and expanding economy with high aggregate demand and increasing employment. This combination is highly conducive to strong corporate earnings and positive equity market performance.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 YoY growth > 2% AND UNRATE 3-month change < -0.2%
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are negative or flat in more than 50% of periods where both GDPC1 growth is strong and UNRATE is falling.
